source.title: Protein-altering and regulatory genetic variants near GATA4 implicated in bicuspid aortic valve

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Bicuspid aortic valve (BAV) is a heritable congenital heart defect with unknown genetic origins. Previous studies (linkage, candidate sequencing, small GWAS) failed to identify robust genetic associations. The goal was to discover genetic variants associated with BAV using a GWAS approach enriched for protein-altering variants, followed by replication and functional validation.

section_gists.heading: Protein-altering and regulatory genetic variants near GATA4 implicated in bicuspid aortic valve
section_gists.gist: Introduction: BAV is the most common cardiovascular malformation (~1% prevalence), moderately heritable (20-89%), and associated with serious complications (aortic stenosis, dissection). Previous genetic studies (linkage, candidate sequencing, one small GWAS with 68 cases) failed to identify significant loci except NOTCH1 in <6% of cases. The study aims to perform a larger GWAS with protein-altering variant enrichment to discover new BAV-associated variants.
process_signals: Prior GWAS with 68 cases found no genome-wide significant results, motivating larger sample size.
Hypothesis: BAV has a complex genetic basis with multiple variants of incomplete penetrance.
Focus on protein-altering variants due to enrichment on genotyping array.
methods: Genotyping array: Illumina Human CoreExome (498,075 variants, 43.8% protein-altering)
Replication in up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls
failures_or_limits: Previous GWAS (n=68) underpowered, no significant findings.
NOTCH1 variants explain only a small fraction of BAV.

section_gists.heading: Results
section_gists.gist: GWAS discovery identified a low-frequency intergenic variant rs6601627 (MAF=4.1%, OR=1.9, P=3.0e-15) 151 kb from GATA4, and a common missense variant p.Ser377Gly in GATA4 (rs3729856, MAF=14.5%, OR=1.31, P=8.8e-8 after replication). The two variants are independent (LD r2=0.013). Functional follow-up: chromatin conformation (Hi-C, ChIA-PET) showed looping between the noncoding variant region and GATA4 introns; luciferase assays suggested enhancer activity; GATA4 disruption in iPSC-derived endothelial cells impaired endothelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EndoMT), a key process in valve development. A rare missense variant in DHX38 (p.T1221M) also reached genome-wide significance but was not further studied.
process_signals: Discovery of genome-wide significant noncoding variant near GATA4 led to hypothesis that GATA4 is the causal gene.
Missense variant in GATA4 selected for replication due to proximity and protein-altering nature.
Conditional analysis confirmed independence of the two GATA4 variants.
Functional experiments designed to test regulatory mechanism of noncoding variant and role of GATA4 in valve development.
EndoMT impairment by GATA4 disruption supports its role in BAV.
methods: GWAS discovery: logistic regression with age, sex, PCs as covariates
Replication: in silico in additional cohorts
Chromatin conformation: Hi-C and ChIA-PET data from ENCODE
Luciferase reporter assays for enhancer activity
CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4 in iPSCs, differentiation to endothelial cells, EndoMT assay (collagen gel)
metrics: LD r2=0.013 between the two variants
ExAC: GATA4 pLI=0.8, missense variant predicted benign/tolerated
EndoMT: GATA4 sgRNA reduced mesenchymal cells to ~30% of control
failures_or_limits: No significant eQTL detected for rs6601627 in GTEx, suggesting effect may be developmental or modest.
Missense variant rs3729856 predicted benign/tolerated by PolyPhen-2 and SIFT, CADD score 9.418 (top 11% deleterious).
Functional mechanism of noncoding variant not fully established; only suggestive chromatin looping and enhancer activity.

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: The study identifies genome-wide significant variants near GATA4 associated with BAV. The noncoding variant may affect GATA4 expression via chromatin looping, potentially disrupting EndoMT. GATA4 is a known cardiac transcription factor; mutations cause various congenital heart defects. The missense variant p.Ser377Gly also associates with tetralogy of Fallot (ToF), suggesting pleiotropy. The noncoding variant is specific to BAV. Limitations include incomplete functional validation, European-only discovery cohort, and modest sample size for rare variants.
process_signals: Hypothesis: noncoding variant disrupts a regulatory element critical for GATA4 expression during valve development.
Missense variant may have broader cardiac effects (associated with ToF).
Acknowledged need for future functional experiments to confirm mechanism.
Population-specific variant frequencies noted (e.g., noncoding variant rare in East Asians).
methods: Look-up of GATA4 variants in ToF cases
Comparison of variant frequencies across populations (1000 Genomes)
metrics: p.Ser377Gly associated with ToF (not quantified in text)
Noncoding variant rs6601627: 108/115 correlated variants have MAF<0.01 in East Asians
failures_or_limits: Cannot confirm mechanism of noncoding variant; only suggestive chromatin interaction.
Functional experiments not performed in developing heart tissue.
Discovery cohort limited to European ancestry; generalizability uncertain.
Sample size limits power for rare variant discovery.
Incomplete penetrance and complex genetics acknowledged.

section_gists.heading: Methods
section_gists.gist: Detailed methods: GWAS genotyping on Illumina Human CoreExome array, imputation to HRC panel, QC (sample and variant filters), case-control matching (age, sex, ethnicity), association testing with logistic regression. Replication in multiple cohorts. Functional assays: chromatin conformation data from ENCODE, luciferase assays, iPSC generation and CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4, endothelial differentiation, EndoMT assay.
process_signals: Stringent QC to avoid batch effects (same array, same center).
Greedy matching algorithm for controls to maximize ancestry matching and power for rare variants.
Use of publicly available functional genomics data (GTEx, ENCODE) to prioritize GATA4.
iPSC and CRISPR methods to test GATA4 function in vitro.
methods: Imputation: SHAPEIT2 phasing, minimac3 imputation to HRC r1.1
QC: sample call rate >98%, variant call rate >98%, HWE P>1e-5, cluster separation >0.3, imputation r2>0.3
Association: logistic regression (EPACTS, PLINK) with age, sex, 4 PCs
Replication: IMPUTE2 imputation, SNPTEST logistic regression
Functional: Hi-C, ChIA-PET, ChromHMM, luciferase, iPSC-CRISPR, EndoMT
failures_or_limits: Potential for residual confounding despite matching.
Functional assays in cell lines may not fully recapitulate in vivo development.
No direct evidence of variant effect on GATA4 expression in developing heart.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: Initial hypothesis: BAV has a complex genetic architecture with multiple common and rare variants.
GWAS discovery of noncoding variant near GATA4 shifted focus to GATA4 as causal gene.
Missense variant in GATA4 provided additional support, though predicted benign.
Functional experiments aimed to link noncoding variant to GATA4 regulation and GATA4 to valve development via EndoMT.
Pleiotropy of missense variant (BAV and ToF) suggests different mechanisms for coding and noncoding variants.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Exact causal variant(s) and mechanism by which noncoding region affects GATA4 expression.
Whether the noncoding variant acts in developing heart valves in vivo.
Functional significance of the missense variant p.Ser377Gly despite benign predictions.
Contribution of other loci (e.g., DHX38) to BAV.

events.summary: BAV is heritable but genetic origins remain elusive; previous GWAS underpowered.
Study design: GWAS with 466 cases and 4,660 controls, replication in up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls.
Hypothesis that noncoding variant influences GATA4 expression in developing fetal heart or with modest effect.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers abstract, introduction, and early results including GWAS design, discovery of GATA4 variants, independence, and initial functional hypothesis.

events.summary: Identified rs118065347 as a likely functional variant because it co-localizes with multiple transcription factor binding sites and is in a known enhancer active in fetal heart.
Chromatin interaction loops from ChIA-PET and Hi-C data show that the region containing rs118065347 loops to an enhancer near GATA4, suggesting potential regulatory interaction.
No evidence for heterogeneity in association at GATA4 between BAV cases with or without TAA, suggesting BAV impacts TAA risk through altered hemodynamics rather than shared molecular mechanisms.
CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4 in iPSC-derived endothelial cells impairs EndoMT, shown by reduced SMA expression and fewer migrating mesenchymal cells.
Hypothesis that noncoding variant disrupts a regulatory element affecting GATA4 expression during valve development, possibly impairing EndoMT.
Cannot yet confirm mechanism of action of noncoding variant on aortic valve development; functional experiments needed.
Population frequency analysis shows noncoding variant rs6601627 and correlated variants are rare or monomorphic in East Asians, indicating population specificity.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains functional annotation of candidate variant, chromatin interaction analysis, phenotypic heterogeneity analysis, ToF association, iPSC-CRISPR EndoMT experiment, hypothesis, limitation, and population frequency analysis.

events.summary: GATA4 deficiency impairs endothelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EndoMT), a critical step in valve formation.
numbers_or_metrics: GATA4 sgRNA reduced mesenchymal cells to ~30% of control

events.summary: Western blot confirms GATA4 protein reduction in sgRNA-treated endothelial cells.
Western blot shows reduced SMA expression in GATA4-deficient cells undergoing EndoMT.
Immunofluorescence staining shows reduced mesenchymal marker SMA and endothelial marker CD31 in GATA4-deficient cells.
GWAS genotyping and imputation pipeline designed to avoid batch effects and maximize power for rare variants.
Greedy matching algorithm used to select ancestry-matched controls for each case.
iPSC generation and CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4 to study valve development.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains detailed methods and functional validation experiments for GATA4 role in EndoMT, as well as GWAS design and analysis details.

events.summary: Replication cohorts were assembled and genotyped to validate the three variants identified in the discovery GWAS.
entities: University of Texas Health Science Centre cohort
numbers_or_metrics: 305 BAV cases and 2,746 controls from MHI
452 BAV cases and 1,634 controls from Partners
152 BAV cases and 874 controls from UT Health
275 BAV cases and 1,686 controls from ASAP-ARTIST-POLCA-Olivia

events.summary: Association analysis was performed in each replication cohort using logistic regression with covariates.
Quality control and imputation were performed on replication cohort genotypes.
BioMe replication analysis included genetic matching and stratification by ancestry.
BioMe replication had few non-European cases, so results were presented excluding them.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk describes replication cohort details and methods, but does not contain results or functional experiments. It provides process events related to replication study design and analysis.

events.summary: CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4 in iPSCs and EndoMT experiments were performed.
Chromatin conformation analysis was performed to investigate regulatory mechanisms.
Statistical analysis of association data was conducted by multiple researchers.
The study was designed by C.J.W. and B.Y.
Phenotypes of samples were characterized by multiple researchers.
Biological mechanisms were discussed by M.O., J.K., Y.E.C. and B.Y.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk primarily contains acknowledgements and author contributions, with limited process-specific events. Extracted events are based on explicit statements of performed tasks.

evidence_bank.quote: Despite the prevalence, importance and heritability of BAV, its genetic origins remain elusive.
Only one previous genome-wide association study (GWAS) for BAV has been published in a limited number of cases (n=68; ref. 14), which did not identify any genome-wide significant results.
here we perform an unbiased genome scan in a large study of BAV cases (n=466) and controls (n=4,660), with replication in additional samples of up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls.

We propose that this noncoding variant, or a variant tagged by it, influences GATA4 expression in a manner not detectable by GTE—either exerting an influence on gene expression levels only in the developing fetal heart or with a relatively modest effect that was not detectable in the current GTEx sample size.

we identified rs118065347 as a variant likely to be functional because it co-localizes with binding regions for multiple transcription factors (including KAP1, CCNT2, CJUN, C-MYC, GATA2, HDAC2, HMGN3, JUND, MAX, SP1, TAL1, YY1 and ZBTB7A) and is in a known enhancer active in fetal heart, left and right ventricle, right atrium, as well as other tissues
The variant falls inside a ChIA-PET loop connecting to a region also annotated as an enhancer 3' of GATA4 and C8orf49 and 5' of NEIL2.
These data indicate that this distal region is brought in close proximity to GATA4 and disruption of this region may have direct impact on GATA4 expression.

we saw no evidence for heterogeneity between BAV cases with or without TAA (Supplementary Table 3) and between BAV cases with or without a positive family history of BAV and/or TAA (Supplementary Table 4), suggesting that BAV probably has an impact on the risk of TAA because of altered haemodynamic blood flow and aortopathy from different mechanisms instead of sharing molecular mechanisms with TAA
The GATA4 sgRNA group showed significantly fewer mesenchymal cells migrating out after 3 days than control cells

chromatin conformation experiments suggest that the region near the associated variants appears to loop and physically interact with regions intronic to GATA4. This hypothesis could be tested in future functional experiments to investigate whether the noncoding BAV-associated variants identified here affect expression of GATA4 at a critical time in heart development. This could possibly disrupt EndoMT, a process important for normal trileaflet aortic valve formation.
Although we cannot yet confirm the mechanism of action of the noncoding variant(s) on chromosome 8 on aortic valve development
among the noncoding variant rs6601627 and its 115 correlated variants, 108 variants have MAF<0.01 and 73 are monomorphic in East Asians
We demonstrated that deficiency of GATA4 impaired the EndoMTs, a critical step in valve formation

Immunofluorescence staining of SMA and CD31 of the control and GATA4 sgRNA undergoing EndoMT.
To avoid any potential batch effects, cases and controls were genotyped using the same array in the same genotyping centre
We opted for this approach to provide the best ancestry-matching between cases and controls
sgRNAs were designed to target GATA4 exon2 (the first coding exon) with sgRNA design tool

In the Partners HealthCare cohort, 452 Caucasian BAV cases were identified from the electronic medical records (EMRs) of Partners HealthCare
In the University of Texas Health Science Centre cohort, 765 patients with sporadic TAAs or aortic dissections were collected and genotyped.
Three cohorts ASAP, ARTIST and POLCA were included in this replication group with a total of 275 BAV cases and 1,686 controls used for analysis.
In total, there were 41 BAV cases with available genotyping data

Multidimensional scaling was used to detect and exclude population outliers.
For each case, three controls were selected by genetically matching using the first two genetic principal components and stratification by age and sex.
We performed analyses both including and excluding the BioME non-European samples, and results were highly similar.
We present results in this study excluding the non-European samples since there were few cases.

Many mutations in GATA4 have been previously reported to be found in different kinds of CHDs atrial septal defect $^{32-34,54-58}$ , ventricular septal defect $^{32,34,57,59,60}$ and ToF $^{34,57,61}$ , although mostly tested in family studies.

Furthermore, the GATA4 mutations have been identified in CHD patients with various ancestries: European $^{32-34,58}$ , Asian $^{32,54,55,57,59-61}$ and Native and Hispanic American $^{34}$ .

We first identified potentially functional variants using RegulomeDB and HaploReg in the region near rs6601627 or variants in high LD ( $r^{2}>0.6$ ; ranging from rs112197605 to rs117851931; hg19:chr8:11774952–11838697) $^{41,42}$ .
For example, among the noncoding variant rs6601627 and its 115 correlated variants, 108 variants have MAF<0.01 and 73 are monomorphic in East Asians $^{62}$ .

BAV is associated with serious consequences: 30–70% of those with BAV will develop dilated thoracic aorta $^{3}$ ; 15–71% of BAV patients develop aortic valve stenosis depending on age group and individuals with BAV have a 50-fold higher risk of severe aortic valve stenosis $^{4}$ ; and up to 47% of BAV patients develop aortic valve incompetence $^{5}$ .

We performed variant-level quality control (QC) by excluding 22,983 variants that met any of the following criteria: variants with a cluster separation score $<0.3$ , $<98\%$ genotype call rate or deviation from Hardy–Weinberg equilibrium $(P<1\times10^{-5})$ .

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Bicuspid aortic valve (BAV) is a heritable congenital heart defect with unknown genetic origins. Previous studies (linkage, candidate sequencing, small GWAS) failed to identify robust genetic associations. The goal was to discover genetic variants associated with BAV using a GWAS approach enriched for protein-altering variants, followed by replication and functional validation.

canonical_events.summary: BAV is heritable but genetic origins remain elusive; previous GWAS underpowered (n=68) with no significant findings.
Study design: GWAS with 466 cases and 4,660 controls, replication in up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls, using Illumina Human CoreExome array enriched for protein-altering variants.

GWAS discovery identified a low-frequency intergenic variant rs6601627 (MAF=4.1%, OR=1.93, P=3.0e-15) 151 kb from GATA4, and a common missense variant p.Ser377Gly in GATA4 (rs3729856, MAF=14.5%, OR=1.31, P=3.2e-6 in discovery).
Replication in multiple cohorts confirmed association of rs6601627 and rs3729856; rs3729856 reached near genome-wide significance after replication (P=8.8e-8).
Identified rs118065347 as a likely functional variant co-localizing with multiple transcription factor binding sites and in a known enhancer active in fetal heart.
Chromatin interaction loops from ChIA-PET and Hi-C data show that the region containing rs118065347 loops to an enhancer near GATA4, suggesting potential regulatory interaction.
Hypothesis that noncoding variant disrupts a regulatory element affecting GATA4 expression during valve development, possibly impairing EndoMT.
CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4 in iPSC-derived endothelial cells impairs EndoMT, shown by reduced SMA expression and fewer migrating mesenchymal cells (reduced to ~30% of control).
No evidence for heterogeneity in association at GATA4 between BAV cases with or without TAA, suggesting BAV impacts TAA risk through altered hemodynamics rather than shared molecular mechanisms.
Population frequency analysis shows noncoding variant rs6601627 and correlated variants are rare or monomorphic in East Asians, indicating population specificity.
Cannot yet confirm mechanism of action of noncoding variant on aortic valve development; functional experiments needed.
BioMe replication had few non-European cases, so results were presented excluding them; generalizability to non-European populations uncertain.
Missense variant rs3729856 predicted benign/tolerated by PolyPhen-2 and SIFT, CADD score 9.418 (top 11% deleterious); functional significance unclear.

edges.reason: The problem of underpowered previous GWAS motivates the design of a larger GWAS with replication.
The GWAS design enables the discovery of associated variants.
Discovery of the noncoding variant leads to identification of a likely functional variant.
Identification of rs118065347 enables chromatin conformation analysis to investigate regulatory interactions.
Chromatin looping evidence supports the hypothesis that the noncoding variant affects GATA4 expression.
The hypothesis motivates functional experiments to test GATA4's role in EndoMT.
The functional experiment shows GATA4's role but does not confirm the mechanism of the noncoding variant, leading to the limitation.
Replication in BioMe reveals limited non-European cases, leading to the limitation.
Discovery of the missense variant leads to the limitation regarding its predicted benign nature.
Discovery of GATA4 association enables analysis of heterogeneity with TAA.
Discovery of rs6601627 enables population frequency analysis.

must_cover_points: Problem: BAV heritable but genetic origins elusive, previous GWAS underpowered.
Design: GWAS with 466 cases, 4,660 controls, replication in larger cohorts, protein-altering variant enrichment.
Replication: Confirmed associations, rs3729856 P=8.8e-8 after replication.
Independence: Conditional analysis shows LD r2=0.013.
Functional variant: rs118065347 in enhancer with TF binding sites.
Chromatin looping: ChIA-PET/Hi-C shows interaction with GATA4 region.
Hypothesis: Noncoding variant affects GATA4 expression, impairing EndoMT.
Experiment: GATA4 disruption impairs EndoMT (mesenchymal cells ~30% of control).
Limitation: Mechanism of noncoding variant not confirmed.
